evidence.verifiedDetail: Completed a real one-turn check — sent a prompt and got a model reply (strongest proof)
evidence.liveDetail: This engine ran in a recent real task (live evidence; no separate turn check)
evidence.launchDetail: Only confirmed the process starts; no turn was run — a logged-out / bad-key / out-of-quota engine only fails when a turn is actually attempted

backend.title: Backend stopped
backend.statusStopped: The local backend process is no longer running.
backend.statusPermanent: Auto-restart gave up — restart it manually.

mcp.title: MCP
mcp.pageSubtitle: Install MCP servers for your agents · applies to all workspaces · stored on this machine
mcp.nodeMissing: Node.js not detected — npx-based MCP servers (incl. chrome-devtools) can't run. Install Node.js LTS first.
mcp.realWriteHint: Toggles write directly to claude / codex MCP config (user scope, applies to all workspaces' agents). More MCP servers coming.
mcp.userScopeWarning: These toggles write to user-level claude / codex config. Once enabled, agents flockmux spawns afterward (captain and workers) inherit and can call these MCP servers; already-running agents pick them up only after being recreated. Enable or disable only when you trust this local project.
mcp.enableDesc: This writes to the CLI's user-level config and applies to agents in every workspace. Enable only when you trust this local project and MCP server.
mcp.disableDesc: This removes the MCP from the CLI's user-level config and affects agents in every workspace immediately; agents relying on it may lose capability. You can re-enable it later.
mcp.keyDesc: claude and codex share one key — enter it once; saving syncs to both enabled CLIs.
mcp.keyUnset: No key set (enter once when enabling; shared by both)
mcp.scopeHint: Installed MCP servers apply to all workspaces; stored on this machine only.
mcp.noMatch: No matching MCP — try “Add custom” below.
mcp.configureDesc: Fill in the required fields, then add. Stored on this machine only.
mcp.customDesc: Fill in by hand or import from JSON. Stored on this machine only.
mcp.secretWarn: Secrets are stored in this browser's localStorage only — do not commit them.

jsonErr.json: Couldn't parse JSON — check the format
jsonErr.shape: Couldn't find a server config — check the structure

mcp.nodeTooOld: Node.js {{version}} is too old — npx-based MCP servers (incl. chrome-devtools) need Node {{min}}+ (LTS). Please upgrade before enabling.
mcp.opOkRefreshFailed: Operation succeeded, but refreshing the status failed — the toggles shown may be out of date

catalogPurpose.filesystem: Read and write files within allowlisted directories
catalogPurpose.git: Read, search, and edit local Git repositories
catalogPurpose.github: Repos, issues, PRs, Actions, and code scanning
catalogPurpose.context7: Inject up-to-date library and framework docs into prompts
catalogPurpose.chrome-devtools: Drive and inspect live Chrome, performance, and network
catalogPurpose.fetch: Fetch a URL and convert it to markdown
catalogPurpose.postgres: Postgres with health checks, index tuning, and EXPLAIN
catalogPurpose.sentry: Inspect errors, issues, traces, and performance
catalogPurpose.brave-search: Web, local, image, and news search
catalogPurpose.aws: AWS API, docs, EKS, ECS, and IaC

shell.cmdkHint: Press {{shortcut}} to open the command palette

welcome.title: Get Claude, Codex, OpenCode & Reasonix working as a team on your project
welcome.subtitle: Wire up the AI CLIs you already use into one crew — tell them what you want, they split the work, talk to each other, and ship it.

step1.title: Create a workspace
step1.body: Bind a real project directory so the team starts with the right code, files, and boundaries.

step2.title: Tell the AI the goal
step2.body: Describe the feature, bug, or refactor in plain language and let the captain break it down.

step3.title: Track progress from multiple angles
step3.body: Chat, DAG, ledger, and replays stay in sync so you can inspect status, evidence, and terminal work without guessing.

chat.title: Group chat
chat.body: Every agent is a member, mention and reply like Slack

spell.title: Adaptive orchestration
spell.body: The captain plans as it goes — spawning the workers each task needs and wiring up their dependencies on the fly, no flowchart required

auto.title: Auto dispatch
auto.body: Describe the task in plain English; the captain breaks it down and starts the right agents

welcome.sidebarBackendDown: Backend unreachable — workspaces can't load right now

tour.dialogDesc: Get to know flockmux in 30 seconds

sidebar.title: Your workspaces live on the left
sidebar.body: One workspace = one project + a crew of AI agents. Run several at once and swap with the sidebar; open multiple parallel 'directions' under one workspace, isolated from each other.

tabs.title: Four views on the same workspace
tabs.body: Chat for messages, DAG for who's waiting on whom, Ledger for task progress + the shared blackboard, Replays for each agent's terminal. ⌘1–4 jumps between them.

composer.title: Tell the AI what you want — plain English
composer.body: "Add a login page", "fix this bug" — the captain breaks it down and spins up the right agents to do it.

cmdk.title: ⌘K opens the command palette
cmdk.body: Search commands, switch workspaces, wake stuck agents, change themes — almost everything is one keystroke away.

common.networkError: Can't reach the local service (127.0.0.1:7777). Make sure flockmux is running.

merge.title: Merge "{{name}}" to main
merge.intro: This direction changed {{count}} file(s); merging into {{base}}
merge.noChanges: This direction has no changes to merge
merge.baseDirty: The main direction ({{base}}) has uncommitted changes — commit or stash them first
merge.conflictBody: {{count}} file(s) conflict — an AI is reconciling them; check back in chat
merge.cleanupHint: This direction is merged — you can wrap it up.

error.title: This view hit an error
error.body: The rest of the app still works. Retry, or reload the page.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Preferences live in this browser; model / plugin config lives on the server

general.title: General
general.hint: Global behaviour, mostly takes effect immediately
general.openMainHint: When on, the chat window expands automatically; off keeps it in the tray
general.desktopNotifyHint: Fires a desktop notification whenever an agent replies to you
general.failureHint: Cascading behaviour when one agent in a spell fails
general.killOthersHint: When on, if any agent exits abnormally the others in the same run are stopped too

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.hint: Switch light / dark / system instantly; preference stored in this browser

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
shortcuts.hint: All hardcoded today; full custom binding waits for a global cmdk-driven table

plugins.title: CLI plugins
plugins.hint: Loaded from cli-plugins/*.toml; the server checks whether each real CLI is executable and shows official install commands when missing.
plugins.probeHint: "Installed" only means the command resolves; click to actually start each engine and confirm it can run.
plugins.probeRunning: Starting each engine for real, this can take a few seconds…

privacy.title: Privacy & Security
privacy.hint: Local data export / clear, shim tool-call mode
privacy.approvalModeHint: Currently shim launches CLIs with --dangerously-skip-permissions, so all tool calls pass through; product direction is to keep bypass always-on, no human-in-the-loop approval mode planned
privacy.exportHint: Serialise every flockmux:* localStorage key as a downloadable JSON
privacy.clearHint: Remove all flockmux:* localStorage keys (language / theme / notification read state / settings). Current window runtime is not affected; reload to fall back to defaults
privacy.clearConfirm: Really clear? This can't be undone.

about.title: About
about.endpointHint: Loopback port flockmux-server listens on (localhost only)
about.cratesHint: 9 crates make up the backend; src-tauri is the desktop shell

update.title: Software update
update.hint: Check for and install new versions — never automatic, your call
update.desktopOnly: Updates are available in the desktop app only

models.title: Model allocation
models.hint: Configure which model each CLI uses and how hard it thinks. opus/sonnet/haiku are Claude's own tiers (its --model accepts those aliases); other CLIs (e.g. codex = gpt-5.x) don't have them — just set their default model.
models.noOverride: This CLI does not support a --model override (its plugin declares no model_args); it picks its own model.
models.effortHint: Default reasoning depth for this CLI's agents (Claude --effort / Codex reasoning effort); override per direction in chat.
models.effortCodexNote: Codex caps at “xhigh”; picking “max” auto-downgrades to xhigh.
models.unsavedLeaveDesc: You changed the model config but haven't saved. Leaving will discard these changes.

bootstrap.stepFirstResponse: Waiting for the captain's first response
bootstrap.slowHint: Taking longer than usual, hang tight…

orchestratorFailure.title: The captain can't start yet
orchestratorFailure.titleTimeout: The captain started but didn't respond
orchestratorFailure.titleEscalated: Still failing after retries — needs you

emptyState.greetingTitle: Hand a task to the captain
emptyState.greetingBody: Say what you want done. On send, the captain comes online, breaks it into a plan, delegates to members, and reports back here.

starter.refactor: Refactor this function, extract the reusable logic
starter.test: Add failing-case tests for this code
starter.bug: Help me find the root cause of this bug

emptyState.noEngineBody: Install and log in to at least one AI engine before the captain can start.

chat.emptyStateHint: Name the workspace, point at the project folder — AI scouts it for you
chat.globalScopeHint: Showing data from every workspace · pick one in chat to scope this view
chat.selectWorkspace: Pick a workspace to see its members
chat.noMembers: No AI is online here right now.
chat.workspaceStripCliMissing: No AI engine is available yet. Install Codex or Claude before auto-wake can work.
chat.workspaceStripCliInstallHelp: Settings shows official install, verify, and login commands.
chat.workspaceStripCliPartial: Work can continue with {{names}}; missing {{missing}} can be added later.
chat.workspaceStripReviveHint: Sending a message starts the role's AI; missing providers fall back automatically.
chat.newDirectionTooltip: Open a new direction (git projects auto-isolate, won't touch main)
chat.newDirectionNamePlaceholder: Name it (optional — the AI names it from your first message)
chat.newDirectionSpawnNote: Opening a direction immediately starts a new captain for this direction.
chat.newDirectionNonGit: This project isn't a git repo — directions share the same files (no isolation), so their edits will clobber and affect each other.
chat.directionDegraded: Isolation failed — sharing the main directory (files may clobber each other)
chat.directionDeleteConfirmBody: Stops every AI running in this direction and removes its isolated copy (if any). Main and other directions are unaffected.
chat.manageRootsHint: Attach the source of dependency / tool projects so AI reads it directly instead of decompiling. flockmux writes a marked, managed block into the primary project's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md pointing AI at this source (auto-removed when you detach).
chat.rootPathPlaceholder: Paste the absolute path of a dependency source dir
chat.deleteConfirmBody: Every agent in this workspace will be stopped. This can't be undone.
chat.feedOffline: Live connection lost — showing last known state

vanishedTurn.body: {{role}} exited this turn without producing a reply — possibly an expired login, a restart, or an abnormal interruption, so your last message wasn't delivered.

chat.directionIsolationDegraded: Direction isolation failed; fell back to sharing the main directory
chat.newDirectionIncomplete: Direction creation didn't finish, please retry

a11y.nodeDefault: Press Enter or Space to select a node, arrow keys to move, Esc to cancel.

dag.title: Flow
dag.subtitle: {{count}} live agent(s) · edges = handoff deps
dag.empty: No live agents. A spell will populate this graph.

interruptAll.title: Pause all agents?
interruptAll.desc: This interrupts every running agent in this direction and stops auto-wake until you resume them.

resumeAll.title: Resume all paused agents?
resumeAll.desc: This resumes each paused agent in this direction and lets them continue working.

dag.interruptAllPartialFail: {{n}} agents failed to pause, please retry
dag.resumeAllPartialFail: {{n}} agents failed to resume, please retry

replays.title: Replays
replays.unscopedHint: This recording's agent has no workspace (e.g. launched directly via MCP, or the agent row was cleaned up).

player.notFoundHint: Could not find a recording with id {{id}}. It may have been deleted, or the URL is wrong.
player.liveTruncatedHint: Still recording — incomplete: playback only goes up to the bytes flushed so far and may cut off abruptly at the end. Reopen after it finalizes to see the full recording.

context.title: Context board
context.subtitle: {{count}} key(s) · read + history (written by agents)
context.selectKey: Pick a key on the left
context.rightHint: Pick a key to see metadata and history

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.subtitle: from /ws/swarm · {{count}} unread
notifications.markAllReadConfirm: Mark all {{count}} unread notifications as read? This can't be undone.
notifications.openFailed: Couldn't locate the space this message belongs to; opened the notification center instead

wake.title: Wake {{role}}?
wake.desc: This sends a manual wake message so the agent reads its mailbox / blackboard. Use it only when the agent is actually stuck or needs a human nudge.

pause.title: Pause {{role}}?
pause.desc: This sends Ctrl-C to interrupt the current turn and makes auto-wake skip this agent until you resume it.

resume.title: Resume {{role}}?
resume.desc: This restores auto-wake for the agent and sends one manual wake so it can continue working.

agent.terminalExited: This agent has exited — its terminal is gone. See what it did in the Recordings / Messages / Context tabs.
agent.noRecordings: This agent has no recordings yet
agent.noMessages: No messages to or from this agent

activity.emptyHint: Once this agent starts working, every step — tool calls, file reads and writes — shows up here in real time.

agent.noBlackboard: This agent hasn't written to the blackboard
agent.injectPlaceholder: Send the agent a note; it'll be delivered on the next wake
agent.deadNoAction: This agent has ended; no actions available
agent.sheetDesc: PTY terminal / recordings / messages / context for agent {{id}}
agent.sheetDescAcp: ACP activity / recordings / messages / context for agent {{id}}

acpNoTerminal.title: This AI runs over ACP — there's no terminal screen
acpNoTerminal.body: opencode is driven over a structured protocol (ACP), not inside a terminal. See its live actions (reads, commands, thinking, replies) in the Activity tab.

wizard.title: Create workspace
wizard.subtitle: Pick the primary project first; AI prepares the context
wizard.errDirMissing: That directory doesn't exist — check the path is correct and points to an existing absolute path.
wizard.errBackendNotReady: The local service isn't ready yet — please retry in a moment; if it keeps happening, restart flockmux.
wizard.step2Hint: AI works from this directory. Dependency source and multi-project context can be attached later.
wizard.dirPlaceholder1Tauri: Paste an absolute path, or click Browse… on the right
wizard.dirPlaceholderMore: Attach a peer / dependency / tool project's source dir (optional)
wizard.dirHint: AI will work inside this folder
wizard.attachedHint: AI reads this source directly (no decompiling)
wizard.projectHint: Peer project — AI develops it too (not a dependency)
wizard.advancedClosedHint: Multi-repo projects, dependency source, and tooling live here. You can skip this the first time.
wizard.advancedOpenHint: Attach extra source by relationship so AI can read it as context.
wizard.pickFolderUnavailable: Available inside the desktop app; paste the path manually in browser-dev mode
wizard.defaultInfo: After you hit Start, AI scouts your project first
wizard.scanningHint: AI is scanning the project directory you just picked to figure out the stack and project type. When it's done it'll greet you in chat and ask what you want done.
wizard.scanningFootHint: Usually 20-40s — you can enter the room and wait
wizard.pathNotADirectory: That path is a file, not a folder — enter the folder your project lives in.
wizard.pathPermissionDenied: You don't have permission to access that directory — pick one you can read, or grant access in your system settings.
wizard.attachedErrorCount: {{count}} attached directories have problems — expand to fix

messages.composerPlaceholderEmpty: No AI members in this workspace yet…
messages.composerPlaceholderRevive: Send a message — the AI spins up automatically…
messages.queuedToCaptain: Queued · delivered when the captain is free
messages.attachFailedAria: {{name}} failed to upload, click to retry
messages.modelConfirmBody: The in-flight reply will be interrupted and the captain will restart on the new model.

reasoning.stepOrchestrator: Checked the direction and workspace state, then chose whether to answer directly or dispatch workers
reasoning.stepWorker: {{role}} read the task brief and relevant context
reasoning.stepCheckedThread: Compared this round with existing progress to avoid duplication or gaps
reasoning.stepPlanning: Planning the execution path and next action
reasoning.stepExecuting: Executing, waiting on tool results, or shaping the reply
reasoning.stepAnswered: Turned the work into the final user-facing reply

messages.recipientPicker: Send to someone in this room…
messages.sendHintMobile: Enter adds a new line · tap Send to send
messages.optimizeTooltip: Let AI rewrite your input to be clearer (keeps technical details, invents nothing)
messages.truncatedNotice: Content too long — rendering the first {{n}} characters
messages.jumpNotVisible: #{{id}} isn't in this conversation (it may belong to another room/direction, or has scrolled out of range)
messages.jumpUnreadNone: No more unread messages in this conversation

cron.title: Scheduled jobs
cron.subtitle: Deliver a prompt to a workspace's captain on a 5-field cron; "Run now" fires immediately.
cron.localTimeNote: Times are in your local zone {{tz}}
cron.exprHint: Type a 5-field cron expression, or pick a preset above.
cron.invalidExpr: Invalid expression — 5 fields, each in range (min 0-59, hour 0-23, day 1-31, month 1-12, weekday 0-7).
cron.noUpcoming: Valid, but won't fire within a year — check the date combination.
cron.name: Name
cron.promptPlaceholder: What the captain should do when it fires…
cron.empty: No scheduled jobs yet. Fill in an expression and prompt above to create your first.
cron.previewFailed: Couldn't validate the expression (preview unavailable) — you can still try to save.
cron.noWorkspaces: No workspaces yet. A scheduled job needs a workspace to deliver its prompt to.
cron.toggleFailed: Failed to toggle the schedule, please retry
cron.deleteFailed: Failed to delete the job, please retry

files.title: File browser
files.previewHint: Pick a file on the left to preview
files.jailBlocked: Outside this workspace — turn on "Browse whole filesystem" to view.
files.workspacesLoadFailed: Failed to load workspaces — please retry
files.browseAllWarning: Removes workspace isolation — lets you browse the whole machine. Use with care.
files.noWorkspace: Select a workspace to start browsing

tasks.title: Task board
tasks.subtitle: Every worker is a task; status is derived from lifecycle automatically — or mark blocked / done / archived by hand.
tasks.empty: No tasks yet — they appear once the captain spawns workers.
tasks.overriddenHint: Status set by hand, overrides the derived value

blocked.title: Mark "{{role}}" as blocked?
blocked.desc: This writes a manual status and overrides the derived lifecycle status until you reopen it.

done.title: Mark "{{role}}" as done?
done.desc: This writes a manual status and overrides the derived lifecycle status until you reopen it.

archived.title: Archive "{{role}}"?
archived.desc: Archived tasks move out of the normal task flow. Reopen clears the manual override.

reopen.title: Clear manual status for "{{role}}"?
reopen.desc: The task will return to lifecycle-derived status after this.

tasks.statusFailed: Failed to update status, please retry

goals.title: Goals
goals.subtitle: Track the objective, acceptance criteria, status, and token budget for long-running agent work.
goals.objectivePlaceholder: Ship the feature upgrade without moving Claude onto paid SDK/API transport
goals.criteriaPlaceholder:
- Claude stays on interactive PTY by default
- Goal API and UI are tested
- Full workspace tests pass
goals.empty: No goals yet — create one to give the run a durable target.
goals.evidencePlaceholder: What proves progress, completion, or a blocker?
goals.evidenceKindHint: Kind: note for progress · proof of completion · blocker for obstacles
goals.budgetTooLarge: Budget too large — enter an integer no greater than 1 trillion
goals.budgetNotNumeric: Digits only (no commas, spaces, or letters)
goals.loadFailed: Failed to load goals, please retry
goals.createFailed: Failed to create goal, please retry
goals.statusFailed: Failed to update status, please retry
goals.evidenceLoadFailed: Failed to load evidence, please retry
goals.evidenceAddFailed: Failed to add evidence, please retry

usage.title: Usage / Cost
usage.subtitle: Token usage scraped from each worker's session log; cost is estimated from a built-in price table (CLIs don't report spend over the PTY).
usage.trustHint: Prices are estimated from the server's built-in table; unknown models count tokens only. Click an agent row to inspect it in the workspace drawer.
usage.costAtLeast: some models unpriced — real cost is at least this much
usage.pricingFallback: Models not listed fall back to the LiteLLM price table ({{count}} models covered)
usage.confirmResetDesc: This deletes the current user pricing config and uses the server defaults again. Config path: {{path}}
usage.empty: No usage yet — token counts and cost appear here once workers run.
usage.chartTableCaption: Daily token usage (bucketed by UTC day)

terminal.confirmDesc: This opens a real shell for "{{workspace}}" and runs commands you type in that workspace directory.
terminal.backendDown: Can't reach the backend (127.0.0.1:7777), so the terminal can't open. Make sure the flockmux service is running.

model.label: Model
model.tooltip: Choose which model this direction's AI uses
model.heading: Model for this direction

hint.opus: Most capable, slow & pricey — hard problems

model.effortHaikuNote: Haiku ignores thinking effort — no effect on Haiku agents (still applies to other models)

effortHint.max: Maxed out — for hard problems (Codex has no max → X-High)

cmdk.confirmWakeDesc: This sends a manual wake message and nudges the agent to continue. Use it only when it actually needs a manual wake.

blackboard.deleteConfirmDesc: "{{path}}" will be removed from the shared board. This cannot be undone.
blackboard.connectFail: Can't reach the local service (127.0.0.1:7777). Make sure flockmux is running.
blackboard.discardConfirmDesc: “{{path}}” has unsaved content; {{target}} will discard these changes.
blackboard.alreadyExists: “{{path}}” already exists — opened it for you (not overwritten)
blackboard.approvedInfo: Wrote approval marker design.approved · waiting for the agent to act on it
blackboard.rejectedInfo: Wrote rejection marker design.rejected · waiting for the architect to act on it
blackboard.diskChangedWarn: ⚠ {{path}} changed on disk (op={{op}}) — you have unsaved changes
blackboard.approveTitle: Approve this design — writes design.approved, wakes frontend and backend
blackboard.rejectTitle: Request a redo — writes design.rejected with a reason; the architect will redraft
blackboard.rejectReasonPlaceholder: Rejection reason (one line) — the architect reads this and revises
blackboard.selectFileHint: Select a file on the left

debug.noAgentsHint: No agents yet — enter a task above and click ✨ Auto, or launch a single CLI
debug.wakeAgentHint: Wake manually (click this when an agent is stuck — sends it a system message to continue)

ledger.title: AI Work Ledger
ledger.subtitle: The captain's thinking lives here. Task ledger (goals + plan) on the left, progress on the right.
ledger.compactHint: Compact the ledger with a small model (keeps key info; the old version stays recoverable in the op-log)
ledger.taskEmpty: Nothing yet. The captain creates this on its first wake.
ledger.progressEmpty: Nothing yet. The captain updates this live as it assigns work.
ledger.breadcrumbsSubtitle: Recent worker heartbeats (written automatically after each step)
ledger.breadcrumbsEmpty: No worker heartbeats yet. A line appears here when a dispatched worker hits a milestone (install / build / writing code, etc.).

home.backendUnreachableDesc: The flockmux backend (127.0.0.1:7777) is not responding, so your workspaces could not be loaded. Make sure the service is running, then retry.

recordings.playTitleLive: Live recordings can be replayed, but only up to the bytes already written
recordings.liveTruncatedHint: ● Still recording — incomplete: playback only goes up to the bytes flushed so far and may cut off abruptly at the end. Reopen after it finalizes to see the full recording.

spells.needWorkspaceToRun: Use this inside a workspace: a spell needs a workspace context to run.
spells.needWorkspaceShort: Use this inside a workspace: a spell needs a workspace context.
spells.taskPlaceholder: What do you want to do? e.g. build a simple password-strength checker
spells.autoTitle: Auto: the planner reads your task and picks a spell for you. The shared workspace config below is ignored — the planner picks one itself.
spells.runTitle: Run the spell selected in the dropdown
spells.advancedTitle: Expand/collapse shared workspace config (only fullstack-style spells need it)
spells.workspaceDirPlaceholder: Shared workspace absolute path (fullstack-style spells only)
spells.workspaceDirTitle: Absolute path. Only takes effect when the spell manifest declares shared_workspace=true. Leave empty to let the server assign one automatically.
